summary: Machine policy for Find My Device. Microsoft documents the same device policy path, value name, default, and numeric meanings that the app writes.

setting.name: Find My Device
setting.casual_explanation: Find My Device can register the device and its location in the cloud so it can be located later. This policy decides whether that feature is allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to avoid cloud registration of the device and its location or because they do not want location-based recovery features.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it reduces cloud registration and location tracking, but it also removes Find My Device recovery features.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents AllowFindMyDevice in the Experience Policy CSP and FindMy.admx maps it to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FindMyDevice\AllowFindMyDevice with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Find My Device disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The device and its location are not registered and Find My Device will not work.

allowed_values.label: Find My Device enabled
allowed_values.meaning: The device and its location can be registered in the cloud so the feature can locate the device later.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowFindMyDevice = 0, which matches the documented disabled state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1703 and later client systems exposing the policy

states.rationale: Microsoft documents the default value as 1.

windows_defaults.notes: By default, the feature is allowed to operate.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want recovery features if the device is lost
avoid_for: Users who do not want location-based cloud registration
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want device-location recovery features to remain available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want the device and its location registered in the cloud.
tradeoffs: Find My Device can help locate the device later.
The feature relies on cloud registration and location handling.

recommended_profiles.label: No cloud device location
intended_for: Devices that should not use location recovery
avoid_for: Portable devices that benefit from Find My Device recovery

states.rationale: Disable the feature and stop cloud registration.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to avoid device-location registration and cloud recovery features.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to be able to locate the device later if it is lost.
tradeoffs: The device and its location are not registered for Find My Device.

evidence.title: Microsoft Experience Policy CSP: AllowFindMyDevice
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the default value, allowed values 0 and 1, behavior, and registry mapping for Find My Device.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FindMy.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the policy to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FindMyDevice\AllowFindMyDevice with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FindMy.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains that enabling the feature allows cloud registration for device-location recovery, while turning it off stops registration and the feature from working.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowFindMyDevice = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - Find My Device policy
evidence.summary: FindMy_AllowFindMyDeviceConfig maps AllowFindMyDevice under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FindMyDevice. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling Find My Device registers the device and its location in the cloud, while disabling it prevents registration and makes Find My Device unavailable. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Microsoft documents AllowFindMyDevice directly in the Experience Policy CSP and maps it to SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FindMyDevice\AllowFindMyDevice with 1 as the default allowed state and 0 as disabled. The app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: This policy turns on Find My Device. ... When Find My Device is off, the device and its location aren't registered and the Find My Device feature won't work. ... Default Value 1; Allowed values: 0 Not allowed. 1 (Default) Allowed. Group policy mapping: Registry Key Name SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FindMyDevice; Registry Value Name AllowFindMyDevice.
validation_proof.notes: The Experience Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes and defines the 0/1 semantics. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-findmydevice-admx.
